text.title: 20 Unconventional Places Near Tokyo You've Never Heard Of - Public Transport Accessible!
text.u: if you've already been to Tokyo you've
probably seen all the main attractions
and now you want something different
well here is 20 things in and around
Tokyo that you've probably never heard
of all accessible in a day trip or less
with public transport this video is
sponsored by Tokyo tourism who suggested
most of these locations but I'm only
including ones that I personally approve
of kicking off the list we've got a
place that I've been to a couple times
now in different seasons and it's
absolutely beautiful and that is toi
Shrine this place is stunning just the
trees and the V and in all weather it's
going to look amazing and in different
seasons completely different but so so
so magical there's a bus that can drop
you off at any of the main points along
the way or you can follow my suggestion
which is to get off at hula bus stop and
then there's like a 3our mini pilgrimage
kind of hike that is just so beautiful
and it visits the five main shrines of
to gaki in Winter the hike won't be
accessible though because it's just way
too much snow but uh you can still get
off the bus at any of the stops along
the way especially the first and the
last one I highly recommend them but the
final one in winter again don't go past
the cedar Corridor because there'll just
be too much snow you won't be able to
access the last Shrine but the corridor
itself is absolutely beautiful and as a
bonus there'll be nobody else there to
get here catch a train to Nagano station
and from there catch a bus for about 1
hour to HW CA bus stop also this is in
my travel guide okay next up we have
zeni and its surrounding area uh this
one is also right next to Nago station
so you could bundle it together with the
to gaki hike or you could just do it
separately on its own this Temple is
massive and really really beautiful
there's actually like a number of
different things to see like in the area
around it the thing that I really love
is this lined path all the way down
towards the temple and there's heaps of
all these really cute really interesting
little shops and restaurants uh the one
that comes to mind in particular is a
shop where you can make your own
Japanese shichimi like hot pepper like
uh powder you get to choose all the
different flavors that you like and then
put it together with the right ratios
and everything and then you get to take
home your own personalized souvenir hot
pepper I really really love this
experience like surprisingly loved it
and it was super affordable as well I
think it only cost me like 800 Yen or
something and the other interesting
thing that we did was we ate some Shin
Yori which if you don't already know
this is a Japanese monks Cuisine very
traditional and there's a bunch of rules
that they need to follow when they make
it like everything has to have at least
like five different colors and five
different flavors honestly I thought I
knew what what kind of food we would be
eating but it blew away all of my
expectations and I really really really
enjoyed it I highly recommend this to
everyone even if you're not vegan or
vegetarian oh yes it's traditionally
vegan Cuisine I should have mentioned
that even if you're not vegan it's so
tasty and so unique and I highly
recommend this also to get here just
take a bus from Nago station about 14
minutes to zenji bus stop so this next
location is one that it feels and sounds
totally inauthentic it sounds like one
of those like who crazy Japan tourist
traps but it's actually 100% real this
is VB bar and simply put it is run by
monks they're real practicing monks
that's a real Shrine in the corner and
they're serving you real alcoholic
drinks the drinks themselves are really
tasty quite unique really fun names as
well in the menu and the food that they
serve like the bar snacks and stuff that
is all Shin Yi which I just talked about
that's the traditional monks Cuisine a
couple of times throughout the night
they'll host like this mini Buddhism
sermon uh in Japanese and in English and
they'll also invite you to partake in
chanting the
Sutra overall it's just a really fun
really wholesome experience and it's
really easy to get to as well you just
need to catch the train to ysia San CH
station right in Tokyo and it's like a
5minute walk from there next up we have
one of the coolest and most fun
experiences I've probably ever had in
Japan and that is something called
yabusame so back in the Kamakura period
they had this kind of archery on
horseback that was used as training for
the Samurai and it's actually still
practiced today at places like Canadian
Camp yatsugatake which is where we went
for our experience so basically you
start off with a lesson on how to shoot
the bows and then a quick lesson on how
to ride the horse and then you get to
try them together honestly I just felt
like a badass it was quite challenging
like especially maneuvering the bow and
your fingers and getting the fingers out
of the way and all of that but I never
in my life thought that I'd get to
experience archery on Horseback very
very cool they'll tailor the experience
to you so whether you're like a
professional Rider or an absolute
beginner beginner Rider they'll choose
the right horse for you and they cater
all of their uh teaching towards your
level as well so even if you're an
absolute beginner you'll still have a
really really great time it'll cost
12,000 yen for the lesson plus you must
have purchased your own travel insurance
from your home country and an additional
4,400 Yen for the traditional attire
which you don't have to wear but the
option is there if you want to to get
there just catch the limited Express
train to kabuchi zawa station and then
the club will send a private car to come
and pick you up alternatively you can
rent a bike from the station and use
that to ride to the club because if you
do that then from there there's a bunch
of other things in the area that you can
see so we went to the Keith hering
Museum there's like a lot of really
famous architecture in the area as well
there we went to like a little Brewery
that had Brew sorry it's really hard to
say Brewer that have really delicious
drinks so if you run to bike then you
get to see all of that in the same area
so yeah it's up to you depends what you
want to do with your day also if you
just so happen to be driving there is
three absolutely amazing spots that I
can't not recommend they're called Mami
inaga lake is the first one and it's
like the most beautiful autum leaves
Lake and there's freaking swans swimming
in it it's so beautiful but there's no
public transport there unfortunately the
other one is called Saka and this is
basically just like a photo location you
can't really do much here but it's like
what a photo and the last one is called
yamataka jindai zakura and this is a
place that has the oldest cherry blossom
tree in the world question mark because
no one really knows but they say it's
about 2,500 years old which is insane
and they also have uh a Cher blossom
tree where they sent the Le the the
seeds to space and then it came back and
it started mutating and I've made a
whole video about it oh my gosh there's
so much to see in this area um but yeah
again like these ones are a little
trickier to get to so only if you're
driving but I've got so many
recommendations oh I could keep going
from kabuchi zawa you're going to need
to take the train back to tachikawa
station anyway so why not stay at the
most beautiful hotel I've ever stayed at
in Japan sod Hotel only opened a couple
of years ago and it is a beautiful
modern take on the Japanese Zen kind of
architecture but the real standout
feature here is the infinity pool on the
top floor the weather was kind of
average when we were here but on a clear
day you can see Mount Fuji even in
Winter because the pool is heated it's a
10-minute walk from tachikawa station
and the price to stay is usually around
42,900 Yen but it can get a lot cheaper
in the offseason unfortunately there's
an additional 2,000 yen to use the
rooftop pool but if you factor that into
the price then it makes it a lot easier
to stomach around the hotel there's a
bunch of like really nice shops and
restaurants and also there is a cinema
that is doing something there that I
have never seen before they have
something that's called explosive sound
and sometimes they'll actually get the
sound designer from the movie itself
that will come in to the cinema and
tweak the audio so that it sounds as
close to the director's Vision as
possible the movie we watched was Civil
War and it was so intense so intense
again this is around a 10-minute walk
from tachikawa station and tickets are
around 2,000 Yen each although not every
movie has the explosive sound so just
double check that when you make a
reservation on the website all of the
applicable links are in the description
down below do you like Mount Fuji well
so do I I don't like hiking Mount Fuji
though cuz it's kind of
boring but I highly recommend hiking a
mountain with a view of Mount Fuji and
this one is probably the most easily
accessible the most doable within a day
trip of Tokyo and that is Mount isi this
mountain is just behind Lake yamanaka
and the views of Mount Fuji are unreal
it is a pointto point 8 to 10 km
depending where you finish uh hike and
there's only about 400 m elevation gain
and it will take most people between 4
to 5 hours without any breaks most
people start their hike at yamanakako
hirano bus stop and is accessible with a
direct bus from Shinjuku that will cost
you 2,350 Yen and take 2 hours and 24
minutes from there the hike starts with
a buttload of stairs and then just like
a regular incline it'll usually take you
about an hour to get to the top of Mount
ishii from there some people will turn
around and go back the same way but I
highly recommend you keep going down to
mount Hi and then Mount or because
they've just got the most vast amazing
uninterrupted views of Mount Fuji that
you'll ever see and then to get down
from here you can go down a number of
different ways but it doesn't really
matter because you'll probably finish
somewhere near the lake and then
there'll be a bus that can pick you up
along the lake anyway most people will
catch the bus to C guci Co station and
then take the train back to Tokyo but it
really depends on where you're going
next some parts are pretty steep going
up and down so just be cautious of that
but overall I think even if you're a
beginner hiker as long as you've got a
reasonable level of Fitness you'll be
totally fine but please take caution
make sure you've got grippy shoes you're
bringing a liter of water the if the
weather is bad don't go all of your
basic hiking safety requirements please
take caution especially in Winter
because there might be snow but overall
have fun be safe and don't forget your
next is an underrated I'm going to say
underappreciated area of the city of
Tokyo and that is Kanda jimoo area they
have a long history of a lot of
universities and schools being nearby so
there is a lot of secondhand books here
some are in English most are in Japanese
but even if you can't read the Japanese
it's kind of just interesting to look
through some of them have lived through
multiple Wars and they're still held
together it's wild this are is also
really famous for new and secondhand
sports gear so now you know where to go
if you forgot to bring your goles in the
area we checked out two very very
interesting cafes and restaurants the
first one is called aabi Cafe and this
is like a Board Game Cafe essentially
heaps of games a lot of them are in
English but the thing that's really
interesting is that if all of the seats
are taken up already you can just
request to join somebody else's group so
yeah if you go here you might end up
walking away with some new friends and
the second place is something unlike any
other experience I've ever had in my
life this is vegetable isaka gy I had no
no idea what we were going to walk into
so if you can keep it a surprise from
your friends I highly recommend that
this place is wild just like maximalism
doesn't even begin to
describe all of the decorations
everywhere from the seats that you sit
on to the little uh squeaky toy you have
to squeak to get his attention even the
people who are working there they're
decked head to toe afterwards they come
out and like we'll say goodbye to you
personally it was a very very
interesting experience the food was good
but like you're not there for the food
clearly uh they do have a lot of vegan
options which is great I'd say overall
it's a little expensive for the food but
very cheap for the experience that you
get 10 out of 10 can't recommend this
place enough to get here just go to
Kanda or jimboo station so next up we
have a very relaxing very chill day on
the west side of Tokyo and that is at
gindai and the botanical gardens so
gindai sets itself apart in like it's
very non-touristy very chill Vibe plus
they also have something called a GMA
ducky ritual and this is where you
basically you write down your wish onto
a plank of wood you give it to a monk
and then they'll take it they pray over
it they burn it and then they chant it's
very interesting very unique experience
I wasn't allowed to film it but it's
almost like meditative just to sit and
listen to them it's on twice a day 11:00
a.m. and 1: p.m. and at least one person
has to buy the little uh wooden plank if
you want to be involved or you can just
stand outside and watch for free overall
it's just a really unique experience
also in the area there's a bunch of
little shops and restaurants they're
really famous for Wasabi and so which
was delicious and just around the corner
we have the gindai botanical gardens
this is a huge Garden I'm shocked that I
hadn't heard of it before but they've
got a variety of probably the largest
collection of the most interesting
unique and sometimes rare Flora from all
around the world and it's only 500 yen
for entry we were there in summer so the
main thing was like the Rose Garden but
probably if you come in Spring that will
be the best time uh it's always changing
really seasonal dependent but when we
were there they had a bonsai exhibition
which was really really cool I love
Bonsai but yeah it's just a really
lovely relaxing area especially if you
want to get away from the crowds away
from people you'll have so much space to
yourself here to get to gindai there's
like a number of different ways but it
usually always involves one train and
one bus I think we caught the train to
chofu station and then from there we
took a bus to gindai and you know
overall it's about an hour from Tokyo
Station and finally we've got a
beautiful relaxing naturous Retreat just
2 hours away from Tokyo at a place
called satal log Japan I think is really
going through like a a sauna Boom at the
moment it's like my third sauna of the
month but this is definitely the most
beautiful it's in a brand new facility
and they've thought of absolutely
everything that you could possibly need
to make your experience worthwhile like
they give you a little Wellness shot
before you get full use of the sauna got
like a cold plunge with water directly
from the river there's like places to
relax they give you the Poncho and the
hat and uh you get like a drink
afterwards as well and then shower and
all the am
everything everything you could need
it's all provided there I don't know I
think it was something about like the
the design of the building and the
nature around the area it just made it
genuinely felt very relaxed afterwards
and I really really enjoyed it it's a
little on the expensive side because
it's like a luxury destination for two
people it'll cost you 22,000 Yen for the
half day experience or for the full day
it'll be 44,000 yen you can add extra
people which will make it a bit cheaper
depending on how many people there are
you may need to figure that out on their
website definitely I would save it more
for like a special or like a romantic
getaway or something like that from
tachikawa station take two trains 1 hour
and 25 minutes to CI station and then
from there it's a 14-minute walk to saog
all of the details and everything are in
the description down below and that's it
that is my list of 20 things in and
around Tokyo you've probably never heard
of H have you ever heard of these places
because I had never heard of them uh
before making this video uh so I I hope
that they're new and interesting and
exciting to you let me know what you
think in the comments down below um yeah
and thank you so much to tcv Tokyo
tourism for sponsoring this video I had
a really great time so I hope you
enjoyed this video um go follow me on
Instagram like And subscribe if you want
to see more and thank you for watching
and I'll see you guys next time bye
